description:
You are an integration developer running a small test-suite against several REST APIs.  
  A single consolidated log file has already been generated and is located at  
  /home/user/logs/api_integration.log  

  Goal  
  1. Inspect the log and isolate every request that returned an HTTP status in the **4xx** or **5xx** range.  
  2. Save those lines—unchanged and in the same order they appear in the original file—to a new file named  
     /home/user/logs/api_errors_20230915.log  
     (the date string “20230915” is fixed—do not substitute today’s date).  
  3. Produce a second file, /home/user/logs/api_error_summary.txt, that lists how many times each unique failing
     status code appears. The summary file must obey **exactly** the following format so that automated tests can
     verify it:

        <status-code> <single-space> <count>
        <status-code> <single-space> <count>
        …

     • Status codes must be sorted numerically in ascending order.  
     • Only failing codes that actually occurred should be present.  
     • Each line must end with a single newline character (no extra blank lines at the end).  
     • Example of the required format (do not copy literally—numbers will differ):

        400 2
        404 5
        500 1

  Hints  
  • Standard text-processing utilities like grep, awk, sort, uniq, etc. will be helpful.  
  • Expect roughly a dozen entries in the source log; not every line will be an error.  
  • Use only paths inside /home/user; you do NOT need sudo.  

  When you are done, two new files should exist:

     /home/user/logs/api_errors_20230915.log
     /home/user/logs/api_error_summary.txt

  Both files must have read/write permissions for the current user.
truth:
<!--
     The following information is strictly for the automated grader.

     1. Original file that already exists when the scenario starts.
  -->
  <initial_files>
    <file path="/home/user/logs/api_integration.log"><![CDATA[
2023-09-15T08:01:22Z GET /customers 200
2023-09-15T08:01:23Z POST /orders 201
2023-09-15T08:01:25Z GET /customers/42 404
2023-09-15T08:01:27Z PATCH /customers/42 409
2023-09-15T08:01:30Z GET /inventory 200
2023-09-15T08:01:35Z GET /orders/310 500
2023-09-15T08:01:36Z POST /payments 502
2023-09-15T08:01:40Z GET /health 200
2023-09-15T08:01:45Z DELETE /customers/42 204
2023-09-15T08:01:50Z GET /customers/99 404
2023-09-15T08:01:55Z POST /orders 503
2023-09-15T08:01:59Z GET /analytics 401
    ]]></file>
  </initial_files>

  <!--
     2. Files that must exist after the agent finishes.
  -->
  <expected_files>
    <file path="/home/user/logs/api_errors_20230915.log"><![CDATA[
2023-09-15T08:01:25Z GET /customers/42 404
2023-09-15T08:01:27Z PATCH /customers/42 409
2023-09-15T08:01:35Z GET /orders/310 500
2023-09-15T08:01:36Z POST /payments 502
2023-09-15T08:01:50Z GET /customers/99 404
2023-09-15T08:01:55Z POST /orders 503
2023-09-15T08:01:59Z GET /analytics 401
    ]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/logs/api_error_summary.txt"><![CDATA[
401 1
404 2
409 1
500 1
502 1
503 1
    ]]></file>
  </expected_files>

  <!--
     3. Permission expectations.
  -->
  <permissions>
    All files under /home/user/logs/ should be readable and writable by user.
  </permissions>
name: task_000000_a2ae1ab1
